primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer, en IFRS 15, le modèle de comptabilisation du chiffre d’affaires applicable à une licence de propriété intellectuelle, c’est-à-dire si la licence confère un droit d’accès ou un droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence and scope rule and selected IFRS 15 because it directly governs revenue recognition for licences of intellectual property, whereas IFRS 3 addresses business combinations and does not govern this revenue-timing issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty: the question is expressly about revenue recognition for a licence granted to a customer, which is directly addressed by IFRS 15 paragraphs B52–B63.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern how to determine the nature of a licence promise and whether revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify implied promises and confirm that activities that do not transfer a good or service are not separate performance obligations, which supports the licence analysis.
These general revenue recognition paragraphs provide the broader timing framework that the licence guidance applies or refers back to.

peripheral_authority.reason: This document concerns business combinations and acquired intangible assets, not revenue recognition for licences granted to customers.

treatment_families.family: Distinct licence timing models under IFRS 15

approaches.label: Right to access intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies this as one of the two licence models when the customer receives access to intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period.

approaches.label: Right to use intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies this as the alternative licence model when the customer receives the right to use intellectual property as it exists at the point the licence is granted.
